name: script_diagnostics
description_lines: Fetch script and shader errors, warnings, info, and hints from the active Godot diagnostics sources.
Targeted file_paths support .gd and .gdshader files. Targeted C# diagnostics are temporarily unavailable; use scan_project: true for C# validation.
scan_project: true scans project .gd and .gdshader files and runs one cancellable incremental dotnet build for the selected C# project or solution graph.

The project C# build writes final assemblies to Fennara's isolated per-project diagnostic output. It does not replace .godot/mono/temp/bin/Debug, update Godot's loaded runtime assembly, or trigger an editor assembly reload.

After Godot's initial editor filesystem scan, the plugin prepares the isolated C# build and warms csharp-ls on a worker thread. C# diagnostic calls wait for this preparation or an LSP recovery warmup without blocking the editor main thread.

The isolated background build still runs for a valid selected C# project when csharp-ls is unavailable; only targeted LSP warmup is skipped. Plugin-owned runtime and diagnostic builds are serialized because they share Godot's intermediate MSBuild output.
If C# source changes while the initial background build is running, that build finishes normally and the next explicit project scan performs one forced refresh.

For targeted .gd files, this also loads and instantiates project .tscn scenes in memory on the Godot main thread with Godot logging captured, then attaches script-related scene-load errors back to the script file with source=scene_load and scene_path.
This scene-load pass is diagnostic only: it does not open the editor UI, run gameplay, or validate arbitrary scene/resource state.
With scan_project: true, scene instantiation is skipped and the result reports scene_load_skipped plus the class of scene-attached errors that may be missed.
After one or more related C# writes, finish the complete edit set and then call script_diagnostics once with scan_project:true. Do not build after every C# write.
Use this after editing GDScript or shaders outside a tool path that already ran diagnostics, or when diagnosing parser/type/reference/shader errors that only appear when scenes are opened.
Do not use script_diagnostics to debug general scene/resource configuration; use scene/resource tools for that.
Targeted calls support at most 5 files; use scan_project for broad script/shader health checks.

file_paths.description: Specific .gd or .gdshader file paths to check in one call. Targeted .cs paths are temporarily unavailable; use scan_project:true for C# validation. Hard limit: at most 5 files per targeted call. Omit when using scan_project. Required unless scan_project is true.

items.description: Path to a .gd or .gdshader file (e.g., 'scripts/player.gd', 'shaders/water.gdshader', or 'res://scripts/player.gd').

scan_project.description: When true, wait for any plugin-owned background C# preparation, scan project .gd and .gdshader files, and run one cancellable incremental dotnet build for the selected C# project or solution graph. If source changed during initial preparation, this scan forces one refresh. C# outputs are isolated per project under Fennara's diagnostic cache, so this compile validation does not update Godot's runtime assembly or trigger editor hot reload. Use once after completing related C# edits. Required unless file_paths is provided.
